mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack signals impending corporate earnings deterioration and consumer spending weakness, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean return over 60 days in >55% of triggered cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with rising INDPRO, or the signal reverses direction during inventory accumulation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces monetary tightening, creating a persistent negative pressure on S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or inflation spikes correlate with positive equity returns in >65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.0%, aggregate demand expansion supports corporate profit growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >58% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes, rapid federal funds rate hikes trigger immediate portfolio rebalancing and liquidity withdrawal, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances during high inflation regimes, or the rate hike signal loses predictive power when CPI exceeds 300.
